target.title: fix(#95840): contextPruning (mode: cache-ttl) never fires on OpenAI models: isCacheTtlEligibleProvider excludes OpenAI, so the idle-gap tool-result firebreak is dead for the highest-volume provider
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

The `contextPruning` `mode: "cache-ttl"` firebreak — which prunes stale tool-results after an idle gap so post-idle requests don't re-cache oversized history — never executed for OpenAI models. The eligibility gate `isCacheTtlEligibleProvider()` in `cache-ttl.ts` only returned `true` for Anthropic, kilocode+Anthropic, and Google providers. OpenAI was excluded with no config override, leaving read-heavy agents on `openai/gpt-5.5` with no idle-gap firebreak at all.

Why does this matter now?

OpenAI is the highest-volume provider. Without this firebreak, idle-gap turns (gap > 5-min cache TTL) re-cache the full grown tool-result history cold. Over a 7-day window the idle-gap cold-input bucket was ~2.9M tokens. The `[Old tool result content cleared]` marker had never appeared in any agent session log — the firebreak was dead code for the most common provider.

What is the intended outcome?

OpenAI providers (`normalizedProvider === "openai"`) are now eligible for cache-ttl context pruning. The TTL-based idle-gap pruning tracks timestamps, not cache breakpoints, so it works correctly with OpenAI's automatic prefix cache (unlike Anthropic's explicit cache breakpoints).

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Kilocode+OpenAI remains ineligible (only `normalizedProvider === "openai"` is added, not the kilocode variant).
- OpenRouter-routed OpenAI models remain ineligible (the provider normalizes to `"openrouter"`, not `"openai"`).
- No new pruning modes or config overrides — this is a targeted eligibility fix, not a provider-agnostic pruning redesign.

What does success look like?

- `isCacheTtlEligibleProvider("openai", "gpt-4o")` returns `true`.
- Read-heavy agents on OpenAI models get stale tool-result pruning after idle gaps.
- All 14 tests in `cache-ttl.test.ts` and `attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts` pass.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The single-line addition to `isCacheTtlEligibleProvider` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/cache-ttl.ts` (appending `|| normalizedProvider === "openai"`).
- The test update in `cache-ttl.test.ts`: removed the `expect(false)` for OpenAI and added a dedicated `"allows OpenAI providers"` test case.
- Verify that kilocode+OpenAI and OpenRouter-routed OpenAI correctly remain ineligible.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #95840

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #95610 — same root cause (OpenClaw's context optimizations assume Anthropic-style explicit cache breakpoints and silently no-op on OpenAI's automatic prefix cache). #95610 covers the prefix-stability half (within-TTL cold re-sends); this PR covers the idle-gap firebreak half (post-TTL re-cache size).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — filed as a community bug report with empirical evidence from production agent session logs.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `isCacheTtlEligibleProvider` returned `false` for all OpenAI models, so the `contextPruning` cache-ttl firebreak never fired for the highest-volume provider.
- Real environment tested: Unit test suite (Vitest v4.1.8) against the `cache-ttl.ts` module and its integration with `attempt.spawn-workspace`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `vitest` via `test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` targeting `cache-ttl.test.ts` and `attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (console output):
  ```
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  14 passed (14)
  Start at  20:48:52
  Duration  4.97s (transform 2.47s, setup 385ms, import 4.15s, tests 147ms, environment 0ms)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: All 14 tests pass — 12 in `cache-ttl.test.ts` (including the new `"allows OpenAI providers"` case) and 2 in `attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts`.
- What was not tested: End-to-end agent session with actual OpenAI model calls and idle-gap pruning. The fix is a provider eligibility flag change; the pruning logic itself is unchanged and already tested for Anthropic/Google paths.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a unit-level fix; full integration proof requires running a read-heavy agent on `openai/gpt-5.5` through an idle gap and verifying the `[Old tool result content cleared]` marker appears in session logs. That requires live API access and is deferred to post-merge monitoring.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Production grep across all agent session logs on 2026.6.6 returned 0 matches for `[Old tool result content cleared]` — confirming the firebreak had never fired for any agent, including Anthropic-eligible ones (which simply weren't read-heavy enough to trigger it).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

Ran the Vitest agent test shard via `test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`, which executed:
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/cache-ttl.test.ts` (12 tests)
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts` (2 tests)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added new test case `"allows OpenAI providers"` in `cache-ttl.test.ts` that asserts `isCacheTtlEligibleProvider("openai", "gpt-4o")` and `isCacheTtlEligibleProvider("openai", "gpt-5.5")` both return `true`.
- Updated `"rejects unsupported providers and models"` test: removed the `expect(isCacheTtlEligibleProvider("openai", "gpt-4o")).toBe(false)` assertion that previously encoded the bug.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The `"rejects unsupported providers and models"` test expected OpenAI to be rejected (`toBe(false)`). After the fix, that assertion was removed and replaced with the new positive test case. Without updating the test, the old assertion would have failed.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added and updated — see above.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — agents using OpenAI models with `contextPruning.mode: "cache-ttl"` will now have stale tool-results pruned after idle gaps, reducing cold-cache input token counts.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No — no new config keys, environment variables, or migrations. The existing `contextPruning.mode: "cache-ttl"` setting now applies to OpenAI providers.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No — this change only affects which providers are eligible for context pruning; no auth, secrets, or network behavior is modified.

What is the highest-risk area?

OpenAI's automatic prefix cache differs from Anthropic's explicit cache breakpoints. The TTL-based idle-gap pruning only tracks timestamps (via `readLastCacheTtlTimestamp`), not cache breakpoints, so it should work correctly. However, if OpenAI's prefix cache interacts unexpectedly with the pruning timestamps, it could cause unnecessary re-caching or missed pruning.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The pruning logic itself is unchanged — only the provider eligibility gate is widened.
- The TTL mechanism tracks idle gaps via timestamps, independent of how the provider implements caching.
- The change is narrowly scoped to `normalizedProvider === "openai"`; kilocode+OpenAI and OpenRouter-routed OpenAI remain excluded.
- All 14 existing tests pass, including integration tests in `attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts`.

target.title: fix(acpx): keep leaked non-openai model out of the Codex ACP thinking slot
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Spawning the Codex ACP agent fails during setup when the fleet's default/primary model belongs to a non-OpenAI provider. `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="codex")` resolves that inherited default (for example `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite`) into the Codex model slot, and the Codex ACP model parser then treats the segment after the `/` as a reasoning effort. The spawn aborts with:

```
spawn_failed: Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported.
Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh.
```

An explicit `thinking=low` does not help, because the model id is mis-routed before the thinking value is applied. Reported in #95780.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause is in the Codex ACP model translation in `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`. The original parser split the ref on `/` and unconditionally treated the second segment as a reasoning effort, so `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` became model `google` and effort `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`, which Codex's reasoning-effort set rejects. Codex keeps `model` (a free string forwarded to the provider) and `model_reasoning_effort` (the closed set `{none, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh}`) as separate fields (`codex-rs/protocol/src/openai_models.rs:40`, `codex-rs/protocol/src/config_types.rs:695`), so a non-OpenAI model id is never valid in the effort slot.

`classifyCodexAcpModelRequest` replaces the old splitter with a closed result:

- `{ kind: "override", override }` for a usable request: `openai/<model>` (prefix stripped), a bare `<model>`, `<model>/<reasoning-effort>`, or a thinking-only override.
- `{ kind: "unsupported", thinkingOverride? }` for a ref that is not a valid Codex model and is not `openai/`-qualified (a foreign `provider/model`, or a malformed `<model>/<bad-suffix>`); it carries any valid explicit thinking forward.
- An `openai/`-qualified but malformed ref (for example `openai/foo/bar`) throws immediately, since the caller explicitly named OpenAI.

The earlier revision then *dropped* every unsupported model at spawn. Review feedback (and the ClawSweeper re-review) correctly pointed out that the spawn planner still knows whether the model was a caller selection or an inherited default: `resolveAcpSpawnRuntimeOptions` in `src/agents/acp-spawn.ts` receives `params.model` (the raw `sessions_spawn` override) before it is collapsed into runtime options. Dropping a caller-selected unsupported model silently hides invalid intent.

This revision preserves that provenance instead of discarding it. `resolveAcpSpawnRuntimeOptions` records whether `params.model` was supplied and threads a single `modelExplicit` flag through `AcpInitializeSessionInput` and the `AcpRuntimeEnsureInput` contract down to the acpx runtime (the flag is stripped again before the acpx delegate, so it never reaches the codex-acp wire). The Codex ACP `ensureSession` then applies provenance-aware policy:

- An inherited leaked default that is unsupported is dropped, so Codex starts on its own default. This is the #95780 case: the value is not anything requested for this spawn.
- A caller-selected unsupported or malformed model fails closed with `ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION` before the delegate is reached, so invalid intent surfaces instead of silently falling back.

A model dropped at spawn must also be kept out of the session's persisted runtime controls. The previous revision dropped the inherited default only from the initial `ensureSession` call, but `runManagerInitializeSession` still persisted the leaked id in `runtimeOptions`, and the first turn replayed it through `applyRuntimeControls` -> `setConfigOption(model)`. Combined with the fail-closed Codex control path, an authenticated first turn then aborted with `ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION` before doing any work (the ClawSweeper [P1] against `src/agents/acp-spawn.ts` plus the manager persistence path). `ensureSession` now reports the effective model it applied on the returned `AcpRuntimeHandle` as a closed `{ kind: "applied", model } | { kind: "dropped" }`, and `runManagerInitializeSession` persists that effective model. A dropped inherited default is therefore never saved into `runtimeOptions` and never replayed as a model control before the first turn. A caller-selected unsupported model still fails closed at spawn and never persists, so the new handle field changes only the inherited-default case.

`setConfigOption` / `acp model` (an explicit runtime control) already fails closed for an unsupported model; that behavior is unchanged. A model dropped at spawn is also removed from the session input via `withCodexSessionModel`, so the raw id never reaches the acpx delegate where `applyRequestedModelIfAdvertised` would throw `RequestedModelUnsupportedError`.

The change stays at the Codex ACP owner boundary plus the generic provenance plumbing in core. The new `AcpRuntimeHandle.appliedModel` is additive and optional; only the Codex ACP backend populates it and only the manager reads it. The Claude ACP sibling has no reasoning-effort slot, so it cannot misroute a model id into a thinking level; this issue (#95780) is specific to Codex.

## User Impact

Codex ACP spawns succeed again when the inherited fleet default is a non-OpenAI model, falling back to Codex's own default model with any explicit `thinking` level honored, and the first turn after such a spawn no longer aborts on a replayed model control. A caller who explicitly selects a model Codex cannot run (a non-OpenAI `provider/model` at `sessions_spawn`, an `acp model google/...` control, or a malformed `<model>/<reasoning-effort>`) now gets a clear `ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION` failure instead of a silent fallback or a mis-routed crash. Explicit `openai/*` selections and `<model>/<reasoning-effort>` overrides are unchanged.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`: 74 passed, including new cases that a caller-selected unsupported (`google/...`) and malformed (`gpt-5.4/ultra`) spawn model fail closed without calling the delegate, that an inherited leaked default is dropped, that an explicit supported model passes through without leaking the provenance flag, and that `ensureSession` reports a dropped inherited default and a supported model as applied on the returned handle.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-config.test.ts`: 21 passed, including a regression that drives `initializeSession` with an inherited non-OpenAI default through the first `runTurn` and asserts the dropped default is neither persisted nor replayed as a model control, plus a supported model that is persisted and replayed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.initialize-session.test.ts`: 4 passed; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`: spawn planner threading.
- `oxlint` on the changed files: clean. `tsgo` core, core test, extensions prod, and extensions test lanes: clean.
- End-to-end before/after against the real runtime: see Real behavior proof below.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: after an inherited non-OpenAI fleet default is dropped at Codex ACP spawn (#95780), the leaked model was still persisted into the session runtime options and replayed as a model control on the first turn, so an authenticated first turn aborted with ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION before doing any work.
Real environment tested: the real `AcpSessionManager.initializeSession` followed by the first `runTurn` (which runs `applyRuntimeControls` before the turn) for the `codex` agent, on the pristine `main` manager-initialization path @2aa9d67635 versus this revision @685905a210. The Codex ACP backend is faithfully stood in at its one control boundary: `ensureSession` reports the inherited default as dropped and `set_config_option(model)` fails closed on the unsupported `google/...` value, matching the real Codex control path. The manager persistence and first-turn control replay run for real, with identical inputs on both trees.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: drive the real `AcpSessionManager.initializeSession` for agent `codex` with an inherited `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` default (no caller selection), then issue the first `runTurn`, recording the persisted session model and whether the first turn completed or aborted.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main @2aa9d67635)
persisted session model after spawn: "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite"
backend received set_config_option model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite
first-turn outcome: first turn FAILED with ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION

# AFTER (this revision @685905a210, identical inputs)
persisted session model after spawn: null
first-turn outcome: first turn completed
```
Observed result after fix: on the pristine path the inherited non-OpenAI default is persisted into the session runtime options and replayed as a `set_config_option(model)` before the first turn, which the Codex control path rejects, so the first turn aborts with `ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION`. After this patch the inherited default is reported dropped on the returned handle, the manager persists no model for that session, nothing is replayed, and the first turn proceeds. The only value that moves between the two runs is the persisted session model (the leaked id becomes none), which removes the first-turn abort.
What was not tested: the Codex ACP backend was stood in at its one control boundary, reproducing the real fail-closed `set_config_option(model)` and dropped-default behavior also shown live in the spawn subsection below; no live provider request was sent and the full build was not run.

### Spawn boundary before/after (captured at the immediately prior revision da31813cf9)

This revision changes the spawn path only by additively reporting the applied/dropped model on the returned handle; the classifier outputs and the codex-acp launch argv below are unchanged from da31813cf9, and the manager proof above is the fresh evidence for head 685905a210. Two runs against the real `AcpxRuntime.ensureSession` class path for the `codex` agent on pristine `main` @da15cf48bf versus @da31813cf9 with only the acpx process-spawn delegate stubbed, so the model classifier, the Codex command translation, the provenance gate, and the session-input handling all stay real.

```
# BEFORE (pristine main @da15cf48bf)
inherited leaked default       model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite provenance=inherited-default
  -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION: "Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported. Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh."; delegate.ensureSession calls=0
caller-selected unsupported    model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite provenance=caller-selected
  -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION: "Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported. Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh."; delegate.ensureSession calls=0
caller-selected malformed      model=gpt-5.4/ultra provenance=caller-selected
  -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION: "Codex ACP thinking level "ultra" is not supported. Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh."; delegate.ensureSession calls=0
caller-selected openai control model=openai/gpt-5.5 provenance=caller-selected
  -> OK; delegate.ensureSession calls=1 delegate.model="gpt-5.5" sessionOptions={"model":"gpt-5.5"}

# AFTER (da31813cf9, identical inputs)
inherited leaked default       model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite provenance=inherited-default
  -> OK; delegate.ensureSession calls=1 delegate.model=absent sessionOptions=absent
caller-selected unsupported    model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite provenance=caller-selected
  -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION: "Codex ACP model "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported. Use openai/<model> or <model>/<reasoning-effort>."; delegate.ensureSession calls=0
caller-selected malformed      model=gpt-5.4/ultra provenance=caller-selected
  -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION: "Codex ACP model "gpt-5.4/ultra" is not supported. Use openai/<model> or <model>/<reasoning-effort>."; delegate.ensureSession calls=0
caller-selected openai control model=openai/gpt-5.5 provenance=caller-selected
  -> OK; delegate.ensureSession calls=1 delegate.model="gpt-5.5" sessionOptions={"model":"gpt-5.5"}
```

### Live codex-acp child-process spawn (captured at da31813cf9)

The same fix on the immediately prior revision driven through the real `AcpxRuntime.ensureSession` with the acpx delegate NOT stubbed, spawning the real `@zed-industries/codex-acp` binary as the `codex` agent. A thin wrapper records the exact launch argv, then execs the real binary. The launch path is unchanged in head 685905a210 (the handle now also carries the applied/dropped report):

```
# LIVE (real codex-acp child process; real AcpxRuntime.ensureSession)
caller-unsupported     model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite provenance=caller-selected
  child spawn: (codex-acp child NOT spawned)
  outcome:     ensureSession THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION: "Codex ACP model "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported. Use openai/<model> or <model>/<reasoning-effort>."

inherited-default      model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite provenance=inherited-default
  child spawn: spawned: codex-acp
  outcome:     ensureSession THREW -32000: "Authentication required"

openai-control         model=openai/gpt-5.5 provenance=caller-selected
  child spawn: spawned: codex-acp -c model=gpt-5.5
  outcome:     ensureSession THREW -32000: "Authentication required"
```

A caller-selected unsupported model fails closed before any child process starts (no spawn). An inherited foreign default spawns a real codex-acp child with no `-c model=` argument, so the leaked `google/...` was dropped and Codex falls back to its own default. An `openai/*` selection spawns with `-c model=gpt-5.5`. Both real spawns then reach codex's own session creation and stop at `Authentication required` because this box has no active Codex session, which is past the model-routing boundary this PR changes.

Fixes #95780

target.title: feat(media): force Telegram image generation as documents
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users receiving `image_generate` results could get generated images through photo-style delivery, which can recompress media and make generated image artifacts less reliable to save, inspect, or forward as original files.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change treats Telegram `image_generate` outputs as document/file media where supported. It keeps the existing direct Telegram delivery behavior and also propagates `forceDocument` through source-reply, block-reply, queued tool-media, and outbound delivery payload paths so the normal active generated-media reply path preserves the document-delivery preference too.

The change is intentionally scoped to delivery metadata. It does not alter image generation providers, media file creation, or non-Telegram channel behavior.

AI-assisted: yes; the final patch was reviewed with focused local tests and type/lint checks.

## User Impact

Telegram users should receive generated image outputs as document/file attachments instead of recompressed photo messages. That makes image-generation artifacts more faithful to the generated file and avoids losing file/document affordances in Telegram.

## Evidence

Local verification on commit `ffb71a9a42b3c9933215dc2141efd0157f03b2a9`:

- `git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <18 changed files>` passed.
- Focused tests passed: 357 tests across:
  - `src/agents/runtime-plan/types.compat.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`

  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts`

Operational note: full local `pnpm lint` was started but the small VM killed the extensions shard with SIGKILL after the core shard passed. `pnpm check:changed` attempted Blacksmith Testbox delegation, but this local environment does not have the `blacksmith` executable installed. target.title: fix(subagents): credit requester-consumed descendant completions
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a lifecycle/delivery accounting gap for background and cron-owned subagent trees: when a requester actually consumes completed descendant subagent output, the descendant run is now durably credited as delivered instead of later being finalized as failed/suspended solely because no separate visible delivery event occurred.

## What Problem This Solves

Requester-owned subagent trees can legitimately consume descendant output without sending a separate descendant-visible delivery event. This is common for cron/background flows where a parent/requester gathers frozen child output, composes its own result, and then delivers that requester result outward. Before this PR, the descendant completion could remain delivery-unproven even though its output had already been consumed by the requester. Later cleanup/finalization could then classify that descendant as failed or suspended, producing delivery state that did not match real behavior. This PR gives the registry an explicit durable signal for that case: requester consumption is delivery evidence only for the exact descendant runs that were consumed, under the matching requester session/run-start window, when the requester path adopts descendant fallback output, so the consumed descendant rows do not later announce or finalize as pending just because the final outward send fails.

## User Impact / Intended Outcome

When OpenClaw can prove that a requester consumed descendant subagent output, the matching descendant run receives durable delivery credit:
- `delivery.status` is set to `"delivered"` for the matched descendant completion;
- requester-consumption metadata is persisted with the run/delivery state;
- cleanup/finalize treats `delivery.status === "delivered"` as completed delivery proof;
- normal visible-delivery paths remain required for completions that were not consumed by a requester.
The matching is intentionally narrow. Credit is only applied when all of these are true:
- the consumed run IDs are explicit;
- the requester session key matches;
- the requester run-start window matches the consuming requester run;
- the consumption kind is known;
- cron fallback credit happens when cron adopts explicit descendant fallback output for the requester result;
- nested subagent announcement credit only covers direct child run IDs that were actually included in the requester-consumed output.

This PR keeps the delivery model explicit:
- if a descendant result was visibly delivered, it is delivered;
- if a requester consumed the descendant output because cron adopted it as the requester result, the descendant is delivered by requester consumption;
- if neither happened, failure/suspension handling still applies.

## Linked Context

- Related issue: #92433 — subagent completion can be silently dropped when an announce handoff reaches a requester transcript but the requester run ends before consuming it. This PR addresses the adjacent durable-accounting problem for completions that *are* consumed by a requester.
- Related issue: #67777 — subagent completion delivery can be lost on direct-announce timeout, drain, or orphan prune. This PR does not implement the full durable inbox/spool requested there, but it narrows one delivery-accounting gap by recording proven requester consumption.
- Related issue: #86537 — suspended final delivery can expire and discard a pending subagent completion. This PR keeps requester-consumed completions from remaining indistinguishable from undelivered completions in finalization state.
- Related issue: #90299 — subagent lifecycle state can report lost active execution context while still surfacing child output. This PR contributes to lifecycle/state consistency when descendant output is actually consumed.
- Related PR: #92934 — required assistant acknowledgement for completion wake handoffs, so transcript commit alone is not treated as requester consumption. This PR complements that by adding durable credit for explicit consumed descendant run IDs.
- Related PR: #35080 — deterministic subagent announce delivery with descendant gating. This PR builds on that direction by making consumed descendant accounting durable.
- Related PR: #44205 — stale descendant cron fallback summaries. This PR keeps the same stale-vs-current discipline for requester-consumed descendant credit.
- Related PR: #95604 — Discord subagent progress feedback. This PR is not a Discord UI change, but accurate delivered/failed lifecycle state is useful to progress/failure-marker consumers.
No exact existing issue/PR was found for `markDescendantCompletionConsumedByRequester` or this exact requester-consumed credit mechanism, so this PR should stand as the focused implementation thread for that behavior.

## What Changes

When OpenClaw can prove that a requester consumed descendant subagent output, the matching descendant run receives durable delivery credit:
- `delivery.status` is set to `"delivered"` for the matched descendant completion;
- requester-consumption metadata is persisted with the run/delivery state;
- cleanup/finalize treats `delivery.status === "delivered"` as completed delivery proof;
- normal visible-delivery paths remain required for completions that were not consumed by a requester.
The matching is intentionally narrow. Credit is only applied when all of these are true:
- the consumed run IDs are explicit;
- the requester session key matches;
- the requester run-start window matches the consuming requester run;
- the consumption kind is known;
- cron fallback credit happens when cron adopts explicit descendant fallback output for the requester result;
- nested subagent announcement credit only covers direct child run IDs that were actually included in the requester-consumed output.

## Implementation Notes

Core registry/runtime changes:
- Adds requester-consumed delivery metadata to subagent registry/delivery state.
- Adds a registry operation for marking descendant completions consumed by requester.
- Persists/restores the new delivery fields.
- Exposes the new runtime operation through the existing registry runtime shims.
Main files:
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.ts`
  - Implements durable requester-consumption credit.
  - Matches by requester session, explicit run IDs, and run-start window.
  - Records requester-consumption metadata and persists the updated run state.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.types.ts`
  - Adds delivery/requester-consumption state fields.
- `src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.ts`
  - Persists and restores the new delivery fields.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-runtime.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.registry.runtime.ts`
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-subagent-registry.runtime.ts`
  - Wires the new registry operation through runtime boundaries.
Requester-consumption paths:
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/subagent-followup.ts`
  - Reads completed descendant fallback replies and returns both formatted fallback text and the consumed descendant run IDs.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`
  - Applies cron fallback consumption credit when cron adopts explicit descendant fallback output, before the final outbound send.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`
  - Credits nested/direct child completions when their output is included in the requester-consumed announcement/wake result.
  - Uses the same latest-child dedupe input for rendering and consumed-run credit so stale child rows are not credited.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts`
  - Keeps latest-child dedupe reusable while preserving caller-specific fields such as `runId`.

## Tests / Verification

Coverage added or extended for:
- positive requester-consumed descendant credit;
- no credit for mismatched requester session;
- no credit outside the requester run-start window;
- no credit without explicit consumed run IDs;
- persisted/restored requester-consumption delivery fields;
- cron fallback credit only after successful outbound delivery;
- cleanup/finalize treating `delivery.status === "delivered"` as proof;
- nested/direct child announcement credit;
- stale-vs-current child dedupe, including a negative assertion that stale child run IDs are not credited when stale output is not rendered.
Primary test files:
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.lifecycle-retry-grace.e2e.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts`
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/subagent-followup.test.ts`
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts`

## Evidence

### Rebase/Squash/Proof

- Common upstream base: `13ecca5408cbc94e66105608bfe770d0fc660d76` (`origin/main`, `fix(telegram): back off session init spool retries`)

### Rebase/Squash/Proof

Current rebased/squashed PR head: `a6677b4d1ad6d58e4902bfb72d33be59b4b81d78`.

Latest pushed commit after same-base rebase and squash: `a6677b4d1ad6d58e4902bfb72d33be59b4b81d78`.

All six coordinated PR branches in this batch were rebased onto the same pinned upstream base commit:



Result:

- 201 focused tests passed.

- Base: `31e941c3fc32a2bbb6aeb0a3ae6f5f46777b2a1b`

- Behavior proof refreshed: cron now credits `markDescendantCompletionConsumedByRequester` when it adopts explicit descendant fallback output, before final direct delivery, so a failed final outward send does not leave already-consumed descendant rows pending. SQLite persistence now derives `pending_final_delivery` only from active delivery statuses (`pending`, `in_progress`, `suspended`), so delivered/requester-consumed rows that retain payload metadata do not rehydrate as pending.

  - `git diff --check` -> passed.

- Push: force-with-lease to `kklouzal/openclaw:p1/requester-consumed-completion-credit-clean` completed.

## Compatibility and Risk

Risk is moderate because this touches subagent lifecycle/delivery finalization, but the implementation is deliberately constrained:
- no broad “assume delivered” fallback was added;
- no delivery status is credited without explicit consumed run IDs;
- matching is scoped to requester session and requester run-start timing;
- cron fallback credit is gated behind explicit adopted descendant fallback output;
- stale nested child rows are explicitly excluded after latest-child dedupe;
- persisted state changes are additive.
The main compatibility consideration is that existing delivery state can simply lack the new requester-consumption fields; restore logic treats those fields as optional.

target.title: Refactor external plugin catalog toward feeds
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## Journey ContextThis is step 1 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). This first PR does the smallest safe prep: it makes the bundled official external plugin catalog look like the feed document we want to host later, while preserving the current local behavior.## What This PR Does- Adds feed metadata around `scripts/lib/official-external-plugin-catalog.json` without changing the existing `entries` payload.- Adds parser/type support for the feed-shaped bundled catalog.- Preserves legacy local catalog parsing for existing unversioned catalog shapes.- Fails closed for unsupported versioned feed wrappers so future schema versions do not silently parse as legacy catalogs.## What This PR Intentionally Does Not DoThis PR does not fetch from `register.openclaw.ai`, add auth, add source profiles, add signed envelopes, add regional selection, add snapshot persistence, change search behavior, or move skills into feeds. Those are separate reviewable slices.## How To ReviewReview this as a mechanical shape change plus compatibility guard. The expected behavior is that existing callers still get the same official external plugin entries from the bundled catalog, but the bundled file is now ready to serve as the local fallback for a hosted feed.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- JSON parse of `scripts/lib/official-external-plugin-catalog.json`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (17 tests at initial PR authoring)  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The feed-shaped bundled fallback is still loaded by existing runtime catalog consumers after the catalog wrapper refactor.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr1` at `44c0884c3edc`, source harness via `node --import tsx`, no network services.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Imported `src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts`.
2. Imported `src/plugins/web-search-install-catalog.ts`.
3. Listed official external catalog entries and web-search consumer candidates from the bundled fallback.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "head": "44c0884c3edc",
  "bundledFeedAccepted": true,
  "officialEntryCount": 74,
  "firstOfficialIds": ["wecom-openclaw-plugin", "openclaw-plugin-yuanbao", "openclaw-weixin", "openclaw-zaloclawbot"],
  "webSearchConsumerCount": 9,
  "webSearchConsumerSample": [
    { "pluginId": "brave", "provider": "brave", "npmSpec": "@openclaw/brave-plugin" },
    { "pluginId": "exa", "provider": "exa", "npmSpec": "@openclaw/exa-plugin" },
    { "pluginId": "firecrawl", "provider": "firecrawl", "npmSpec": "@openclaw/firecrawl-plugin" }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The bundled feed wrapper validates, the existing official catalog returns 74 entries, and a real catalog consumer still resolves web-search install candidates from that feed-shaped fallback.

What was not tested:
Hosted network fetching is intentionally not part of this PR. It is covered by the next hosted-fetch PR.

target.title: feat(heartbeat): add config to preserve silent task results in agent context
target.body: Closes #95811 ## What Problem This Solves The `agents.defaults.heartbeat.ackMaxChars` config affected two unrelated behaviors: * Delivering a message to the user * Building the message history for the bot context These behaviors should be configurable separately. This PR adds a new config field to decouple the behavior. Concretely, this allows users to avoid being notified when the bot completes a heartbeat task while still allowing the bot to retain what it did during that task in memory. ## Why This Change Was Made This adds a config-gated heartbeat transcript mode that preserves the existing default behavior, while allowing users to keep only the final heartbeat result in future model context. Heartbeat poll prompts, tool calls, and tool results remain stripped in that mode, so background tasks can be remembered without notifying the user or bloating replay history. ## User Impact Users can run silent heartbeat tasks that produce useful results without being notified immediately, while still letting the agent remember those results for later follow-up. Existing heartbeat behavior remains unchanged by default. ## Evidence This is after the PR. Before the PR, the bot would just answer that it cannot see any heartbeat task in the history. (Note: Specifically because it ends the task with HEARTBEAT_OK, but this is what I want - the bot compile interesting stuff and only tell me about it if I ask. I don't want it to message me after each run.) Image description: > User message: Was there any interesting result in your heartbeat task from 3 hours ago? > Bot message: Yeah! The 18:06 one had the Sega FY26 sales update — P3 Reload at 2.9M, P5R at 8.6M lifetime, Metaphor at 2.4M, LaD Infinite Wealth 2M, Sonic x Shadow 3M. Plus EA laying off more people ahead of the Saudi Arabia sale. ## Validation * pnpm build: passed * pnpm check: 1 typecheck error, unrelated (was in main when I checked it out?) >src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts:1545:46 - error TS6133: 'sessionStore' is declared but its value is never read. >1545 const { agentId, sessionEntry, sessionKey, sessionStore } = * pnpm test src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts --run: 38/38 passed successfully * pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts --run : 10/10 passed successfully * Actual tests on local install by talking to the bot / looking at the requests' body to confirm all 3 config values behave as expected in practice (they do) NOTE: Human + AI-Assisted code, human tests

target.title: feat(voice-call): support Twilio calls in IE1 and AU1
target.body:
AI-assisted: Codex.

## What Problem This Solves

The voice-call plugin always sends Twilio Calls API requests to `api.twilio.com`, which selects Twilio's default US1 processing Region. Deployments using IE1 or AU1 cannot create, update, hang up, or inspect calls in that Region because Twilio call resources are Region-specific.

Closes #95828.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds one optional `twilio.region` setting with a closed `us1 | ie1 | au1` contract. OpenClaw derives Twilio's current canonical REST hostname internally:

- `us1` or unset -> `api.twilio.com`
- `ie1` -> `api.dublin.ie1.twilio.com`
- `au1` -> `api.sydney.au1.twilio.com`

Twilio requires an Edge label in non-US REST hostnames, but its CDN automatically selects REST API ingress; the Edge label is not a useful user-facing choice here. Keeping only `region` avoids invalid combinations and reduces the config surface.

The provider prepares one API target containing the account-scoped base URL and exact hostname, then reuses it for both form-encoded mutations and status lookups. This keeps each SSRF allowlist coupled to the request host without allowing arbitrary base URLs or wildcard Twilio hosts.

## User Impact

Existing US1 users see no behavior change. Users can opt into IE1 with:

```json5
twilio: {
  accountSid: "ACxxxxxxxx",
  authToken: "<IE1_AUTH_TOKEN>",
  region: "ie1",
}
```

Unsupported regions fail config validation. No migration is needed because the earlier `edge` proposal existed only in this unshipped draft branch.

## Evidence

- `pnpm test:extension voice-call` — 50 test files and 463 tests passed.
- `pnpm check:docs` — documentation formatting, Markdown lint, MDX, i18n glossary, and 4,469 internal links passed with zero errors.
- `pnpm docs:list` — `plugins/voice-call.md` remains discoverable.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` — passed.
- Credentialed IE1 provider proof at commit `368ac48a91729a3475b0397e144a42b59014395e`: production `TwilioProvider.getCallStatus` queried a deliberately nonexistent Call SID through `api.dublin.ie1.twilio.com` and returned `not-found`, validating regional authentication and routing. No outbound call was placed and no call state was modified.

<details>
<summary>Redacted credentialed provider output</summary>

```json
{
  "proof": "Credentialed OpenClaw Twilio IE1 provider GET",
  "commit": "368ac48a91729a3475b0397e144a42b59014395e",
  "region": "ie1",
  "hostname": "api.dublin.ie1.twilio.com",
  "requestUrl": "https://api.dublin.ie1.twilio.com/2010-04-01/Accounts/[REDACTED_ACCOUNT_SID]/Calls/[REDACTED_CALL_SID].json",
  "providerResult": {
    "status": "not-found",
    "isTerminal": true
  },
  "authenticationValidated": true,
  "outboundCallPlaced": false,
  "remoteCallStateModified": false
}
```

</details>
- Final autoreview of the region-only contract — clean, no accepted/actionable findings (0.95 confidence).
- `pnpm build` was attempted against the branch’s original base and reached `build:plugin-sdk:dts`; it failed on errors in untouched `src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.ts`. Current `main` has since changed that file, so PR CI provides the merged-state build proof.
- `pnpm check:changed` could not allocate remote proof because the contributor account does not have access to the Blacksmith `openclaw` organization. No Testbox was created; PR CI remains the broad gate.

Twilio contract references:

- https://www.twilio.com/docs/global-infrastructure/understanding-edge-locations
- https://www.twilio.com/docs/global-infrastructure/using-the-twilio-rest-api-in-a-non-us-region
- https://www.twilio.com/docs/global-infrastructure/create-an-outbound-call-via-rest-api-in-a-non-us-twilio-region
- https://www.twilio.com/docs/global-infrastructure/manage-regional-api-credentials

target.title: fix(telegram): route poll answers into sessions
target.body:
Fixes #89573.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users could vote in a public/non-anonymous Telegram poll sent by OpenClaw, but the vote would never reach the originating agent session.

Telegram sends `poll_answer` updates only for non-anonymous polls, and those updates do not include chat or thread routing metadata. Current main can send polls and already requests/receives the relevant update type, but without a `poll_answer` routing path the user's selected option is effectively display-only.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the missing inbound half of Telegram poll support by recording the poll origin when a public poll is sent, then looking that origin up when Telegram later delivers a `poll_answer`.

The registry is stored in the shared plugin keyed-state store (`telegram.poll-registry`), not a JSON sidecar. Poll origin registration lives at the central `sendPollTelegram` boundary, so both the message action path and the outbound adapter path are covered by the same write.

The inbound handler follows the same routing/auth shape as other Telegram engagement events:

- unknown poll ids are ignored
- bot voters are ignored before chat I/O
- empty `option_ids` retractions are ignored before chat I/O
- existing Telegram sender authorization still runs before enqueueing
- DM `requireTopic=true` still blocks when no topic is known
- forum/topic routing uses the thread id resolved at send time
- store read/write failures are best-effort and do not crash the update handler or fail an already-sent poll

Anonymous poll behavior is unchanged. Telegram does not emit `poll_answer` for anonymous polls, so those remain display-only from the agent's perspective.

## User Impact

Public Telegram polls can now be used as one-tap agent input. When a user votes in a non-anonymous poll sent by OpenClaw, the selected option is routed back into the originating direct chat, group, or forum-topic session.

This does not add or change any config, environment variable, CLI option, default anonymity behavior, governance file, dependency manifest, or release/security review surface.

## Evidence

Current clean pushed candidate: `6b62999d9064add8eaccaee5612bd40fcccc559f`

Base used for current-head validation: `origin/main` at `2e6e17f7c5d3664a76d81d2c967bcf11051cefb3`

Focused local validation on the current head:

```console
env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts
# 5 files passed, 314 tests passed

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json
# exit 0

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules /root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.ts
# all matched files use the correct format

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules /root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.ts
# exit 0

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# clean
```

Behavior covered by tests:

- poll registry stores, retrieves, misses safely, and remains bounded
- public poll send records registry context
- anonymous poll send does not record registry context
- topic-suffixed targets store the resolved `message_thread_id`
- registry write failure does not fail an already-sent poll
- `poll_answer` routes to the originating group/topic session
- authorized DM poll votes route to the DM session
- DM `requireTopic=true` blocks when no topic is known
- missing registry entries are ignored
- unauthorized senders are blocked
- bot voters and retractions are dropped before chat I/O
- registry lookup failure does not crash or enqueue

The earlier Foreman review on the same Telegram poll-answer diff found no implementation or policy blocker. This update rebased the same single-commit diff onto current `origin/main`; focused tests, extension typecheck, touched-file formatting, touched-file type-aware lint, and whitespace checks passed on the new head above.

Remaining proof gap:

- Live Telegram Desktop/Mantis proof is not included in this contributor-supplied update. The prior Mantis run on the old stale head failed to show the expected candidate vote-to-session result, and I cannot self-trigger maintainer-gated Mantis. This PR keeps the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` context and asks maintainers to run the visible proof against the new pushed head.
- Final pushed PR branch SHA: `6b62999d9064add8eaccaee5612bd40fcccc559f`

## Related Work

- #89573 is the canonical open issue for this missing `poll_answer` route.
- #72501 was the original PR for this route. It was closed after the stale sidecar-era version failed proof, and GitHub will not let this contributor account reopen it after the maintainer/bot close. This PR is the current-main v2 replacement.
- #18122 merged Telegram poll send/action wiring, but did not complete the remaining `poll_answer` routing path. This PR preserves the access-control concerns raised around that prior work.
- #17357 was a broader stale poll attempt with unrelated scope. This PR is the narrow follow-up: no dependencies, no new skill, no CLI/API redesign, and no default-anonymity change.
- #95309 is a newer draft candidate for the same #89573 root cause. This PR's v2 takes the sidecar-free keyed-store approach and avoids branch-only JSON migration behavior.

## AI Assistance

Implementation and review were assisted by AI tooling. The final diff was reviewed against current OpenClaw source and validated with the commands above.

target.title: fix(deepinfra): use OpenAI video end point and tag DeepSeek thinking format
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Two user-visible DeepInfra integration problems: **Video generation.** Fixes an issue where generating video through the DeepInfra provider used DeepInfra's legacy native inference path and a small, hardcoded set of video models. Newly released DeepInfra video models (Veo 3.1, Wan 2.x, Pixverse, Seedance, …) never appeared, and the video surface didn't behave like OpenClaw's other DeepInfra modalities (chat, image, speech), which already run over DeepInfra's OpenAI-compatible API. DeepInfra has since shipped an OpenAI-compatible `/v1/openai/videos` endpoint and tagged its video models, so the catalog can be discovered live. **DeepSeek replies.** Fixes an issue where chatting with a DeepInfra DeepSeek model (e.g. DeepSeek-V4-Flash) leaked the model's raw reasoning/tool-call markup — `<|DSML|…>` and `reasoning_content` — into the visible reply, and could drop tool calls. Because DeepInfra serves every model family over a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint, OpenClaw treated DeepSeek as a generic "openai" model and never recovered its DeepSeek-dialect output. ## Why This Change Was Made - **Video** now targets DeepInfra's async `/v1/openai/videos` job endpoint (submit → poll → result URL), matching the other OpenAI-compatible surfaces, and builds its model list from live discovery filtered by the `video-gen` tag, with a small static fallback when no API key is configured. Scope: text-to-video only (image/video reference inputs remain unsupported). - **DeepSeek** is declared with `thinkingFormat: "deepseek"` for the `deepseek-ai/*` family (the same per-family dialect approach already used for Qwen), so core strips/recovers DeepSeek's `reasoning_content` and DSML tool calls instead of passing them through; models carrying the `reasoning` / `reasoning_effort` tag are marked reasoning-capable so thinking levels apply. ## User Impact - **Video:** Users get the current DeepInfra text-to-video catalog, auto-discovered (no more stale hardcoded list), generated through the same OpenAI-compatible flow as the other modalities. No config or workflow change needed. - **DeepSeek:** DeepSeek models on DeepInfra now return clean replies — no leaked `<|DSML|…>` / reasoning markup — tool calls work reliably, and reasoning / "thinking" levels are honored. ## Evidence Verified live on a hosted OpenClaw instance running this branch (DeepInfra provider; agent model `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash`), driven via `openclaw agent`, with outbound HTTP captured at the network layer. **1 — Video uses the new endpoint + tag discovery.** Captured request timeline across two generations: - `GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` → 200 — tag discovery (returns 13 `video-gen` models: Veo 3.1/-fast, Wan 2.6/2.2-T2V, Pixverse-6 / T2V / T2V-HD, Seedance 2.0 / 1.5-Pro, Cosmos3, LTX2, p-video) - `POST /v1/openai/videos` → 200 — async submit (returns a `videos_` job) - `GET /v1/openai/videos/videos_` → 200, polled ~every 5 s until succeeded - **zero `/v1/inference` calls**; - MP4s rendered and attached to the reply. _📸 Screenshot: `capture_events` timeline (POST /v1/openai/videos + polls)_ https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1bc82632-378f-4f90-bf4d-f84ac915ff40 **2 — DeepSeek thinking format.** - `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash` discovery tags: `["chat","prompt_cache","reasoning"]` - `openclaw agent --thinking high` on a reasoning prompt → correct step-by-step answer with **no `<|DSML|…>` / `reasoning_content` in the reply**, even though the DeepInfra response body did contain a `reasoning_content` field (confirmed in the capture) — i.e. it's recovered, not leaked. _📸 Screenshot: clean DeepSeek reasoning reply_ [request.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29214104/request.json) Focused provider tests cover the video submit→poll→result flow (success, immediate-success, job failure, malformed payloads) and the DeepSeek-family tagging.

target.title: fix(session-memory): skip transcript-only assistant rows
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session-memory summaries can duplicate assistant replies when a transcript contains both the real provider assistant message and an OpenClaw transcript-only bookkeeping assistant row with identical visible text, such as `delivery-mirror` or `gateway-injected`. Those bookkeeping rows should not be rendered into memory summaries as user-visible assistant turns.

## Summary

- skips OpenClaw delivery-mirror and gateway-injected assistant transcript rows when rendering recent session content for bundled session-memory
- keeps the real provider assistant response while excluding transcript-only bookkeeping rows
- adds regression coverage for delivery-mirror and gateway-injected rows

## Evidence

- `npx vitest run src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` passes on the rebased PR branch: 1 file, 26 tests
- regression coverage verifies the real assistant reply renders once while a matching `delivery-mirror` row is skipped
- regression coverage verifies `gateway-injected` assistant rows are skipped while the real provider row is kept

## Context

Raw transcripts can include both a real assistant response and an OpenClaw bookkeeping assistant row with identical visible text. The session-memory renderer was treating both as normal assistant replies, which duplicated assistant text in memory summaries.

target.title: fix(bedrock): stop replaying stale signed thinking from completed turns
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

On Amazon Bedrock (Converse), the message converter replays **every** historical signed `thinking` block unconditionally. Over a long, tool-heavy session these signed reasoning blocks from already-completed turns accumulate, and Bedrock eventually rejects the entire request with a Bedrock-specific validation error:

```
Validation error: The model returned the following errors: messages.N.content.M: Invalid signature in `thinking` block...
```

Once this happens the session is effectively bricked: every retry resends the same stale signatures, and recovery never kicks in on the Bedrock path (the validation failure is delivered as an in-stream `{type:"error"}` event rather than a thrown rejection — see #95429 / #95430). The only manual workaround is to physically strip the offending thinking blocks from the persisted transcript and restart.

The native Anthropic provider already guards against this in #94493: it only replays signed thinking when the current request keeps thinking enabled, otherwise it keeps just the **active tool turn's** reasoning and drops the rest. The Bedrock Converse converter never received the equivalent logic.

## Changes

- **`extensions/amazon-bedrock/stream.runtime.ts`** — mirror the native provider. Derive `replayThinkingEnabled` (true for Fable 5, or when the request sends `thinking: { type: "enabled" | "adaptive" }`) and use `findActiveAnthropicToolTurnAssistantIndex(...)`. When replay is disabled, drop signed thinking from completed turns and keep only the active tool turn's reasoning, inserting the `[assistant reasoning omitted]` placeholder when a turn would otherwise become empty.
- **`extensions/amazon-bedrock/thinking-replay.ts`** — a small dependency-free copy of the native `findActiveAnthropicToolTurnAssistantIndex` helper and `ANTHROPIC_OMITTED_REASONING_TEXT`, so the Bedrock converter mirrors the native behavior without growing the public plugin-sdk export surface (kept in sync with `src/llm/providers/anthropic-thinking-replay.ts`).
- **`src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`** — also drop `redacted_thinking` blocks whose `thinkingSignature` payload is missing/blank (aborted stream or corrupted persistence), which otherwise trigger the same invalid-signature rejection on the native path.
- Tests for all of the above.

## Evidence

### After-fix real Bedrock run (the proof the previous review asked for)

Reproduced end-to-end against **real Amazon Bedrock** (`bedrock-converse-stream`, region redacted) with a genuine signed thinking block returned by the model. A completed assistant turn carries a signed thinking block whose signature Bedrock no longer validates — the exact state stale, accumulated cross-turn signatures reach in a long session. Sending it the way the **old** converter did (replay all signed thinking from completed turns) reproduces the create-side rejection; applying this PR's `convertMessages(replayThinkingEnabled=false)` drop logic makes the identical request succeed:

```
LABEL: PRE-FIX  (replay stale signed thinking from completed turn)
RESULT: REJECTED  The model returned the following errors: messages.1.content.0: Invalid `signature` in `thinking` block

LABEL: POST-FIX (drop completed-turn signed thinking, keep visible answer)
RESULT: SUCCESS  stopReason=end_turn  answer='4'

LABEL: POST-FIX (turn was reasoning-only -> placeholder text)
RESULT: SUCCESS  stopReason=end_turn  answer='4'
```

The three cases map 1:1 to the converter branches this PR adds: (1) the old unconditional replay is what bricks the request, (2) dropping a completed turn's signed thinking while keeping its visible content recovers it, and (3) a turn that was reasoning-only falls back to the `[assistant reasoning omitted]` placeholder so turn ordering is preserved. A self-contained, dependency-free repro script (boto3 + `converse`) is available and reruns in a few seconds against any Bedrock account with Anthropic Claude access.

### Production telemetry (redacted) behind the pre-fix observations

From a real fleet running the `amazon-bedrock` provider, the gateway trajectory logs recorded the create-side failure as a `bedrock-converse-stream` error. Counts/dates/provider only — no transcript content:

```
error string (verbatim, as logged): Invalid `signature` in `thinking` block

Invalid-signature request failures  : 145
date range                          : 2026-06-04 .. 2026-06-23
by day                              : 06-04:13  06-05:30  06-21:53  06-22:41  06-23:8
provider / api                      : amazon-bedrock / bedrock-converse-stream  (100%)
models                              : global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8 (102), global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7 (43)
distinct sessions bricked           : 6
```

Every affected session stayed bricked on retry until the documented manual workaround was applied (strip the persisted thinking blocks + reset the session). The session that logged 53 of these failures on 06-21 was only recovered by a manual reset at `2026-06-21T23:37Z`; the most recent one was reset at `2026-06-23T05:05Z`. This is the create-side root cause behind the recovery gap tracked in #95429 / #95430.

### Unit tests on this branch

```
$ vitest run extensions/amazon-bedrock/stream.runtime.test.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  62 passed (62)
```

The 4 new Bedrock cases cover: drop stale signed thinking from completed turns / keep the active tool turn's reasoning / insert the omitted-reasoning placeholder / replay when thinking stays enabled. `oxfmt --check` and the plugin-sdk surface budget (`scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`) are both clean.

## Related

- #94493 — native Anthropic equivalent (this PR ports the same fix to the Bedrock Converse path)
- #95429 / #95430 — recovery side (complementary; this PR addresses the create side that produces the invalid payload)

target.title: fix(openshell): pin local mirror mutations
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens OpenShell local mirror filesystem mutations so mkdir, remove, and rename stay confined to their resolved mount roots during local mirror updates.

## Changes

- Routes local mirror `mkdirp` and `remove` through `fsRoot()` root-confined operations instead of raw host-path `fsPromises` calls.
- Routes same-root local mirror renames through `Root.move()` and preserves cross-root mirror rename behavior with staged, streamed root-confined copies.
- Preserves recursive remove, overwrite behavior, hardlinked source compatibility, executable bits, and cross-root directory replacement semantics.
- Adds focused OpenShell regression coverage for local parent rebind races and cross-root rename compatibility cases.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm test:extension openshell -- --reporter=verbose`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `corepack pnpm lint:extensions`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean

- `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` still fails on an unrelated existing unused `sessionStore` in `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts`.

target.title: fix: avoid EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError after compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a problem where embedded runs could fail with:

`EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`

after a successful automatic compaction.

In some workflows the compaction process rotates or updates the session transcript, but the prompt lock cleanup path could still compare against the previous session file fence and incorrectly interpret the change as an external writer.

The result was:

* session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released
* aborted embedded runs
* workflow interruptions
* false takeover detection

## Changes

* Preserve ownership information for compaction writes performed by the same run.
* Ensure compaction-related writes are published as trusted/owned writes.
* Propagate the effective post-compaction session file to cleanup and hook paths.
* Add regression tests for:

  * threshold compaction
  * successor transcript adoption
  * owned writes
  * external writer detection

## Validation

Passed focused regression tests:

* embedded session lock tests
* compaction hook tests
* session ownership tests

External writer protection remains enabled.

## Risk

Low.

The patch does not relax protection against real concurrent writers.

Only compaction writes performed by the same run are treated as trusted.

target.title: fix(runtime): protect active work during restarts
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes the core runtime failure mode behind the 2026-06-22 topic UX incident: model/tool-triggered gateway restarts could bypass active-work deferral and interrupt live user-facing work, while delegated tasks could remain too quiet or duplicate-prone.

The incident evidence showed repeated controlled launchd-handoff gateway restarts around 16:24, 16:25, 16:29, 16:54, 17:07, and 17:15 while active work existed, including 4-6 active tasks and 2-3 embedded runs. Restart drain timeout was `0ms`, producing stale resumes, delayed/no finals, duplicate assistant finals, and repeated “are we done?” nudges rather than a crash loop.

## Changes

- Protect active work during safe restart requests by downgrading `skipDeferral` unless `interruptActiveWork` is explicitly approved.
- Add restart audit context with requester/reason and active-work counts.
- Route gateway tool/update restart paths through the safer restart coordination shape.
- Make user-facing delegated/session tasks default to visible `state_changes` while preserving silent/not-applicable handling for internal maintenance work.
- Attach duplicate ACP spawn intents to existing active work in the narrow safe case.
- Persist task progress delivery watermarks atomically with task updates so state-change delivery does not regress into repeated/progress-only noise.
- Add regression coverage across restart coordination, gateway restart/update methods, task registry/executor policy, ACP spawn, gateway tool, and context-engine maintenance.

## Evidence

Focused regression suite passed locally:


Results: local lint, type checks, focused task/gateway/tool tests, build, and formatting passed. Latest pushed commit: `0ea98d3eb6e`.

## Notes

This PR changes core runtime behavior. It is intentionally source-only and does not deploy or restart any live gateway.

## Additional verification after ClawSweeper/Mantis follow-up

Follow-up fix pushed for the two ClawSweeper findings:

- Preserved the documented `openclaw gateway restart --safe --skip-deferral` operator bypass by treating boolean `skipDeferral` as active-work interruption approval at the restart RPC boundary.
- Tightened duplicate ACP spawn attachment so requests with explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` start a new run instead of reusing an active run with different timeout intent.

Focused local proof after the follow-up fix:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/restart.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts --reporter=verbose

```

Mantis proof has also been attached in the PR thread:

- Native Telegram Desktop proof run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28079858909
- Raw QA files: https://artifacts.openclaw.ai/mantis/telegram-desktop/pr-95812/run-28079858909-1/index.json

## Additional follow-up: stale Telegram topic session reconciliation

This update adds the source fix for a Telegram forum topic/session wedging as `status="running"` after stuck-session recovery had already aborted/drained the embedded run and the task DB had no running work.

### Added changes

- Reconciles persisted running session rows to a terminal non-running state when no active embedded run and no running task remain.
- Applies that reconciliation from stuck-session recovery, incomplete-turn fallback handling, `sessions.abort` no-active-run handling, and startup stale-running sweeps.
- Sends a neutral fallback when a safety/presend hook blocks a native final for internal filesystem/runtime leakage instead of treating invisible suppression as successful user delivery.
- Adds structured reconciliation logs for session key, old/new status, active-run presence, running task count, reason, and safe-fallback delivery.

### Added proof

```bash
npm test -- src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.integration.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
npm run build
```

Results: focused regression suite passed across gateway, logging, and agent shards; full build passed.

### Added regression coverage

- Stalled Telegram topic key recovery marks the session non-running, releases the lane, and allows the next queued turn to run without manual reset.
- `sessions.abort` reconciles a stale persisted `running` row when no active run exists.
- Safety-hook native final suppression sends a safe fallback and does not deliver the blocked/leaky content.
- Startup stale-running sweep does not mark a freshly resumed session as stale failed.

### Remaining gap

No live Telegram topic smoke was run for this follow-up commit. The test uses the concrete Telegram forum topic key shape and local session/lane/task machinery; live adapter proof can be added by Mantis or maintainer runtime proof if required.




Additional local verification after the persisted recovery metadata contract fix:

```bash

```

Results: formatting and test type checks passed. Latest pushed commit: `8f3d904e85b`.

target.title: [codex] Fix stale active work session recovery
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Telegram topic sessions could remain blocked behind stale active work even when the underlying model call, worker, or active run was no longer making progress. In that state, a lane could be classified as stalled but still fail to release because model-call recovery required an active embedded-run marker.

This meant a topic could stay silent behind a persisted busy/running state or queued work even though recovery should have cleared only that affected lane.

## Why This Change Was Made

The diagnostic heartbeat already tracks meaningful progress and has per-lane recovery plumbing. The gap was that stalled `model_call` recovery was tied to `hasActiveEmbeddedRun === true`, so orphaned model work without that marker was observed but not actively released.

This change makes stalled model calls and orphaned active work recovery-eligible once their last meaningful progress age reaches the configured stuck-session abort threshold. It also aligns JSON task output with the operator inspection projection so stale/lost tasks do not look like real active work forever.

## User Impact

- A stale model/worker lane can be recovered per session/topic without a broad gateway restart.
- One stuck Telegram topic should not keep unrelated topics behind stale active-work state.
- Task JSON output is clearer: stale/lost records are projected as inspection state instead of appearing as live running work.

## Evidence

Validated on a fresh worktree based on current `origin/main`:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/commands/tasks-json.test.ts
```
